key_insight: The paper shows that joint MTP-RL training fails or succeeds based on a tradeoff between a first-order alignment term and a second-order perturbation penalty, and proposes adaptive coefficient calibration to balance them.
engineering_relevance: For reasoning-model training, OCC offers a low-cost way to keep multi-token prediction active without sacrificing RLVR performance, improving training efficiency and stability.
method: Decompose MTP's effect on the RL objective into correlation and penalty terms.
Unify detach, cross-entropy, and policy-loss regimes under one optimization view.
Use Optimal Coefficient Calibration (OCC) to track an online coefficient via a log-probability proxy.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on six competition-level mathematical reasoning benchmarks and consistently matches or exceeds the detach baseline.
watch_item: OCC depends on a proxy-based online coefficient estimate, so its stability may be sensitive to training dynamics and proxy calibration.
paper_title: Joint Training of Multi-Token Prediction in Reinforcement Learning via Optimal Coefficient Calibration
